title: campanula garganica subsp. istriaca
intro: Campanula garganica subsp. istriaca, also known as Istrian bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family that is native to the Dinaric Alps in Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes and other compounds. The health benefits of Campanula garganica subsp. istriaca include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestive health, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable natural remedy for various ailments. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
